deferred_smith_review_queue: Broad checker weakening: collect_repo_lifecycle_paths now permanently skips ALL declaration-only building roots (no capture/, no evidence/, no raw/raw-manifest.json), not just the one transient repair root. Today only building-map-evidence-red-cleanup-0625 is excluded, but the rule is general and forward-acting — any future stuck/held declaration-only building would be silently excluded from lifecycle scanning. Human sufficiency decision needed.

Two distinct repair surfaces were combined (data fix to another already-recorded Building's persisted map + a checker logic change) while the stated trigger named only building_map_graph; the lifecycle edit addresses a separate, anticipatory failure whose existence was not live-confirmed.

The data fix mutates work/building-map.json of a separate recorded Building (checker-module-diet-implementation-0625); additions are grep-backed by that building's own raw evidence (reads as reconstruction, not fabrication), but editing another building's persisted map is a notable act whose semantic correctness is unproven.
GREEN was never live-confirmed (python execution blocked in the QA sandbox); Smith may want to re-run check_profile.py --profile building_automation before treating the RED as resolved.
narrowly_proven: Two repair surfaces were produced and are structurally well-formed: the checker-module-diet building-map.json is valid JSON with added brick_instances/edges whose raw_refs and movement_fact_ref resolve through that building's own raw-manifest.json/link.jsonl/movement_trace.json (QA grep-verified), and the lifecycle checker now contains a declaration-only-root skip confirmed by direct source read.

By manual/static tracing the four building_map_graph resolution paths that drove the original RED (prefix-only target_brick_instance_ref for the qa lanes + unresolved group member_refs) are addressed by the added brick_instances and link edges.
